dataset: OSCAR EMR Seed Data (Synthea-style synthetic Canadian patient demographics)

pi_items.source: seed_patients.sql line 115-122, INSERT statement confirms first_name='Jean-Pierre'
seed_patients.sql line 115-122, INSERT statement confirms last_name='Bouchard'
seed_patients.sql line 119 sets year_of_birth='1965', month_of_birth='06', date_of_birth='30'; verified via Python date calculation
DOB 1965-06-30 calculated to age 60 on 2026-03-07 (today's date from system context)

pi_items.verified_value: Penicillin allergy: medically accurate with anaphylaxis/severe severity. Sulfonamides allergy: medically accurate with skin rash/moderate severity
pi_items.source: Web search: Penicillin anaphylaxis verified as severe reaction (Cleveland Clinic, Mayo Clinic, CDC); Sulfonamide skin rash verified as most common reaction. Pre-existing allergies for Jean-Pierre in seed data: none (clean baseline per setup_task.sh line 21-22)
Web search confirms anaphylaxis is the most dangerous reaction to penicillin (Cleveland Clinic, Yale Medicine, Mayo Clinic). Medically accurate as life-threatening allergic reaction.
README.md line 36 confirms 'severity_of_reaction is 'S' (Severe)'. Database schema uses char(2) with 'M'=Mild, 'Mo'=Moderate, 'S'=Severe.
Web search: rash reported as most frequently observed reaction to sulfonamides, with maculopapular eruption being most common manifestation
README.md line 36 confirms 'severity_of_reaction' field. Task explicitly states 'Moderate' severity. Web search confirms skin rash is typically mild-to-moderate reaction.

pi_items.verified_value: Metformin 500mg twice daily and Ramipril 10mg once daily are medically accurate
pi_items.source: Web search: Metformin 500mg BID verified as standard starting dose for type 2 diabetes (Drugs.com, Mayo Clinic, NCBI). Ramipril 10mg OD verified as standard dose for hypertension (DrOracle, Mayo Clinic, NEJM clinical trial with 10mg OD)
Web search confirms metformin is first-line agent for type 2 diabetes management
Web search: 500mg orally twice a day is standard initial dose for metformin in adults with type 2 diabetes (Drugs.com, Mayo Clinic). Considered low dose, often titrated in 500mg increments.
Task description and README line 22 state 'twice daily'. Web search confirms standard regimen for 500mg starting dose.
Web search confirms ramipril is ACE inhibitor used to treat high blood pressure (Mayo Clinic, DrOracle, NEJM)
Web search: While most patients controlled on 2.5-5mg, 10mg is within recommended range (2.5-20mg for hypertension). NEJM clinical trial (HOPE study) explicitly used 10mg once per day.
Web search: Ramipril is prodrug with long elimination half-life permitting once-daily administration (Mayo Clinic, DrOracle)

setup_oscar.sh line 295 confirms Firefox user.js sets startup homepage to 'http://localhost:8080/oscar/login.jsp'. Also setup_oscar.sh line 7 references 'http://localhost:8080/oscar/login.do' as alternative endpoint.
setup_oscar.sh line 217 confirms 'oscardoc account configured'. Also visible in export_result.sh and task_utils.sh
setup_oscar.sh line 217 confirms 'password: oscar'. SHA-1 hash documented in comment at line 206-207.
setup_oscar.sh line 217 confirms 'PIN: 1117'. Also documented in setup_oscar.sh opening comment line 5.

pi_items.metadata_value: OSCAR EMR is open-source clinical software
pi_items.verified_value: OSCAR (Open Source Clinical Application Resource) is a real, widely-deployed open-source EMR system
pi_items.source: Web search: Wikipedia, Oscar Galaxy, McMaster University OSCAR project, SourceForge. OSCAR is the only widely deployed open-source EMR in Canada, GPL v2+ licensed, used by many clinics and practices.
setup_oscar.sh line 69 pulls 'mariadb:10.5'. export_result.sh uses 'docker exec oscar-db mysql' commands. seed_patients.sql uses standard SQL.

pi_items.verified_value: True - Synthea-style synthetic Canadian patient demographics
pi_items.source: seed_patients.sql line 2 explicitly states 'Synthea-style synthetic Canadian patient demographics'. Jean-Pierre Bouchard is fictional with plausible Canadian details.

privileged_info_summary: Jean-Pierre Bouchard is a 60-year-old male (DOB June 30, 1965, Ottawa, ON) seeded in the OSCAR EMR test database. The task requires adding two drug allergies (Penicillin with anaphylaxis reaction and severe severity; Sulfonamides with skin rash and moderate severity) and two active medications (Metformin 500mg twice daily for type 2 diabetes; Ramipril 10mg once daily for hypertension). All clinical information is medically accurate and realistic. The patient data is synthetic (Synthea-style) but OSCAR EMR is a real open-source Canadian clinical system using MariaDB backend. Default test credentials: oscardoc/oscar with PIN 1117.
